questions.question: 1 (a) Use the thermometer to measure the temperature θ R of the water in the container labelled ‘cold water’. θ R = ......................................................... [1] (b) (i) V is the total volume of water in beaker A. Complete the column headings in Table 2.1. Table 2.1 V /θ / 100 120 140 160 180 200 [1] (ii) • Pour 100 cm 3 of hot water into beaker A. •

parts.answer: correct calculation of  answer given to 2 or 3 significant figures 1 May/June 2025 Question Answer Marks , °C 1 temperatures decreasing 1 all temperatures to at least 1 °C 1 suitable scales (occupying at least ½ the grid) 1 plots correct to  ½ small square AND precise plots good best-fit line judgement, thin, continuous line 1 • some form of insulation • add a lid • shield from draughts • white / shiny surface May/June 2025 Question Answer Marks
normal at 90° at centre of MR AND incident ray at 40°  2°, below MR P1 P2 separation at least 5.0 cm 1 reflected ray below MR and normal incident ray and reflected ray meeting at B within 2 mm
 correctly measured to  2° new reflected ray in correct quadrant 1

 = 40  2° justification to include idea of results being equal within the limits of experimental error (or within 10%) 1 • thin pins • thin lines / sharp pencil • view bases of pins / pins vertical • pins far apart / at least 5 cm May/June 2025 Question Answer Marks 4 MP1 circuit used: correct circuit diagram MP2 measurements made: measure wire diameter AND measure voltage and current OR record voltmeter and ammeter readings MP3 analysis of readings: calculate / work out resistance of wire MP4 performing the investigation: repeat for at least two other diameters of wire MP5 variable kept constant: length / material of wire MP6 table: with columns for diameter of wire, voltage, current and resistance each column must be labelled with both quantity and unit MP7 conclusion: plot a graph of resistance against diameter OR an answer which suggests comparing values of diameter AND resistance in table
